summary: Transforms Logical Iceberg Equality Delete Scan operator to Physical Iceberg Equality Delete Scan operator, enabling Merge-On-Read execution for Iceberg tables with equality deletes.
ra_condition: Input operator type is LOGICAL_ICEBERG_EQUALITY_DELETE_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Underlying table is Iceberg format
Equality delete files are accessible
MOR parameters are valid and initialized
Schema matches between data and delete files
performance_impact: Enables efficient Merge-On-Read processing for Iceberg equality deletes, avoiding full table rewrites or expensive post-scan filtering.
edge_cases: Predicate pushdown limitations on delete files
framework_notes: Implementation Rule matching LogicalIcebergEqualityDeleteScanOperator. Leaf node in plan tree. Does not recurse to children. Generates single physical candidate per invocation.

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical Paimon Scan operator into a Physical Paimon Scan operator, enabling data access from Paimon tables during query execution.
ra_condition: OperatorType == LOGICAL_PAIMON_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Paimon table exists and is accessible
Schema compatibility between logical and physical
Supported predicate pushdown filters
performance_impact: Essential for Paimon queries; performance depends on file pruning and predicate pushdown capabilities
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpression with LogicalPaimonScanOperator. As a leaf operator, it terminates recursion. Distribution/Order enforcement is handled by separate Enforcer rules in the Cascades framework.

summary: Transforms a Logical CTE Consume operator into a Physical CTE Consume operator, finalizing the reference to a defined CTE producer and removing logical child dependencies.
ra_condition: CTE ID must reference a valid, optimized CTE producer
correctness_conditions: CTE ID exists in OptimizerContext
Output schema matches CTE definition
Column reference map is valid
performance_impact: Enables CTE result reuse; avoids redundant computation if CTE is materialized or shared across multiple consumers.
edge_cases: Multiple consumers referencing same CTE ID
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule (IMP). Matches LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME pattern. Generates a leaf Physical OptExpression (no children), relying on CTE ID for data source. Does not trigger enforcers as it has no children to enforce properties on.

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical Iceberg Metadata Scan operator into a Physical Iceberg Metadata Scan operator for query execution.
ra_condition: Input operator type matches LOGICAL_ICEBERG_METADATA_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Iceberg catalog connection is valid
Target table exists and is Iceberg format
User has read permissions on metadata tables
performance_impact: Enables metadata queries; cost is low compared to data scans but depends on metadata volume.
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_ICEBERG_METADATA_SCAN group expressions. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion (handled by separate Enforcer rules). Adds physical candidate to Memo for cost-based selection.

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical MySQL Scan operator into a Physical MySQL Scan operator, enabling the optimizer to generate execution plans for querying external MySQL tables.
ra_condition: True
correctness_conditions: MySQL external catalog is configured correctly
Target table exists and is accessible
Schema mapping between StarRocks and MySQL is valid
performance_impact: Enables federated queries. Performance is network-bound; relies heavily on predicate pushdown to reduce data transfer. Higher latency compared to native OLAP scans.
edge_cases: Large result sets causing memory pressure
framework_notes: Matches OperatorType.LOGICAL_MYSQL_SCAN. Adds PhysicalMysqlScan to the current Group in Memo. As a leaf node, it does not explore children or trigger child enforcers.

summary: Transforms LogicalValuesOperator to PhysicalValuesOperator, implementing constant row generation as a leaf node in the execution plan without requiring child operators.
ra_condition: None - direct 1:1 logical to physical mapping
correctness_conditions: Column references must match output schema
Row data types must be compatible with column definitions
No child operators required (leaf node)
Predicate and projection must be preservable
performance_impact: Optimal implementation for VALUES operator; no alternative physical implementations exist. Efficient for small constant datasets, memory-bound for large row sets.
edge_cases: Large constant datasets may exceed memory limits
NULL value handling in constant expressions
Complex expression evaluation in row values
framework_notes: Leaf node rule - creates OptExpression with empty child list (Collections.emptyList()). Matches LogicalValues pattern in Memo groups. No enforcer insertion needed as no distribution/ordering requirements from children. Rule fires once per LogicalValues group expression during physical optimization phase.

summary: This rule transforms a Logical Iceberg Scan operator into a Physical Iceberg Scan operator, enabling the execution of scans on Iceberg tables within the StarRocks Cascades optimizer.
ra_condition: OperatorType == LOGICAL_ICEBERG_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Iceberg table metadata is accessible
Merge-On-Read (MOR) parameters are valid
Table schema matches query requirements
performance_impact: Enables native Iceberg table access with MOR support; preferred over generic file scans for ACID compliance and schema evolution.
framework_notes: Matches leaf logical operator pattern; generates a single physical alternative without recursing into children. Property enforcement (distribution/ordering) is handled by separate Enforcer rules upstream.

summary: Transforms a Logical Hudi Scan operator into a Physical Hudi Scan operator, enabling the optimizer to select a concrete execution path for reading data from Hudi tables.
ra_condition: Input operator type is LOGICAL_HUDI_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Hudi catalog is accessible
Table schema matches logical plan requirements
Hudi timeline is consistent for the query snapshot
performance_impact: Mandatory implementation for Hudi tables; performance depends on Hudi file format efficiency, predicate pushdown, and partition pruning capabilities.
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule (IMP). Adds a physical alternative to the Memo Group containing the LogicalHudiScan. As a leaf node, it does not enforce properties on children but may require enforcers above it.

summary: Converts a Logical Repeat operator into a Physical Repeat operator, enabling native execution of grouping sets or rollup logic within the StarRocks execution engine.
ra_condition: Input operator must be LogicalRepeatOperator with valid children pattern
correctness_conditions: Logical operator semantics must be preserved
Physical operator must support specified grouping IDs and columns
Child plan must be valid and compatible
performance_impact: Preferred over emulation via Union All + Aggregate; reduces overhead by handling grouping set expansion in a single pass.
edge_cases: Complex grouping sets with overlapping columns
framework_notes: Standard Cascades ImplementationRule. Matches LogicalRepeat in Memo group. Does not enforce properties itself; property enforcement is handled by separate EnforcerRules if PhysicalRepeat has requirements.

summary: Transforms a LogicalProjectOperator into a PhysicalProjectOperator, enabling execution of column projection and expression evaluation logic.
ra_condition: Child expression E must have at least one valid physical implementation.
correctness_conditions: Column references in map exist in input schema
Child operator is physically implemented
Projection expressions are side-effect free
performance_impact: Minimal CPU overhead; standard implementation for projection with no alternative strategies (e.g., no hash vs merge).
edge_cases: Type casting or aliasing in column map
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_PROJECT over PATTERN_LEAF. Adds PhysicalProject to Memo. Property enforcement (Sort/Exchange) handled by separate Enforcer rules, not this rule.

summary: Transforms a LogicalMetaScanOperator into a PhysicalMetaScanOperator, enabling the execution of metadata table scans within the query plan.
ra_condition: OperatorType.LOGICAL_META_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Input operator must be LogicalMetaScanOperator
Target metadata table must be accessible
Schema compatibility between logical and physical definitions
performance_impact: Mandatory implementation for metadata access; no alternative scan strategies (e.g., index vs full) provided in this rule.
framework_notes: Leaf node rule in Cascades memo; matches GroupExpressions with LogicalMetaScan; does not recurse to children as scans have no inputs.

summary: This is an abstract base class for all physical implementation rules in StarRocks Cascades optimizer, not a concrete rule. Implementation rules convert logical operators to physical operators with higher priority (promise=2) than transformation rules.
ra_input_pattern: Logical Operator (σ, π, ⋈, γ, τ, etc.)
ra_condition: Matches specific logical operator patterns defined in concrete subclasses
correctness_conditions: Input logical operator must match rule pattern
Required physical properties must be satisfiable
Child groups must have valid physical implementations
performance_impact: Implementation rules determine execution strategy. Higher promise (2) ensures physical plans are considered before transformation rules explore more logical alternatives.
edge_cases: Missing statistics may lead to suboptimal plan selection
Property enforcement may add hidden costs (Sort/Exchange)
Base class cannot be instantiated directly
framework_notes: Base class for all ImplementationRules. Concrete subclasses match specific group expressions in Memo. Enforcer insertion happens when required properties don't match provided properties. Promise value 2 ensures implementation rules fire before transformation rules (promise 1) in Cascades search.

summary: Transforms a Logical EXCEPT operator into a Physical EXCEPT operator, enabling the execution of set difference operations while preserving logical properties like limit and projection.
ra_condition: OperatorType.LOGICAL_EXCEPT with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF children (compatible schemas)
correctness_conditions: Child schemas must be union-compatible (same column count and types)
NULL values must be treated as equal for comparison
Distributed execution requires consistent hashing on comparison columns
performance_impact: Enables MPP execution of EXCEPT; performance depends on shuffle overhead and hash table memory; sort-based alternative may be preferred for pre-sorted inputs
edge_cases: Handling of NULLs in set comparison
High cardinality leading to memory spill
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_EXCEPT in Memo groups; Physical operator defines required distribution properties which may trigger Exchange enforcers on children; does not directly calculate cost but provides candidate for costing

summary: Transforms a Logical Window Operator into a Physical Window Operator, enabling the execution of analytic functions with specified partitioning and ordering strategies.
ra_condition: Input operator must be LogicalWindowOperator with valid child plan
correctness_conditions: Window function semantics (partition, order, frame) must be preserved
Input data must satisfy distribution requirements (hash/shuffle) if specified
Input data must satisfy ordering requirements if sort-based execution is used
Projection and predicate pushdown must remain valid
performance_impact: Enables analytic query execution. Hash-based partitioning reduces sort overhead for un-ordered windows but increases memory usage. Sort-based is standard for ordered windows. Skew handling impacts parallelism.
edge_cases: Window functions without ORDER BY clause
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_WINDOW operator in Memo. Generates PhysicalWindowOperator as a candidate implementation. While the rule itself creates the operator, the PhysicalWindowOperator's requirements (distribution/ordering) typically trigger EnforcerRules (Sort/Exchange) upstream in the Cascades framework.

summary: Transforms a Logical Elasticsearch Scan operator into a Physical Elasticsearch Scan operator, enabling execution against external ES clusters within the Cascades optimizer framework.
ra_condition: Target table is configured as an Elasticsearch external table; Operator type is LOGICAL_ES_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Elasticsearch cluster is accessible and healthy
Index mapping matches StarRocks table schema
Pushable predicates are compatible with ES Query DSL
performance_impact: Enables federated query capability; performance heavily depends on network latency and the efficiency of predicate pushdown to ES.
edge_cases: Empty ES index returns zero rows
Non-pushable predicates evaluated post-fetch increase network traffic
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions containing LogicalEsScanOperator in the Memo. Adds PhysicalEsScanOperator to the same Group. Distribution/Order enforcement is handled by separate Enforcer rules wrapping this operator if required properties are not met.

summary: Transforms LogicalTopN operator to PhysicalTopN operator, providing the standard physical implementation for TOP-N queries with ordering, limit, offset, and partitioning support in StarRocks Cascades framework.
ra_input_pattern: λ(τ(R)) - Logical TopN with ordering over any child relation
ra_output_pattern: PhysicalTopN - Physical implementation of TopN operation
ra_condition: Input must be LogicalTopN operator with valid OrderSpec, limit, offset, and partition configuration
correctness_conditions: OrderSpec must be valid and compatible with input schema
Limit must be non-negative integer
Offset must be non-negative integer
Partition columns must exist in input schema
Predicate must be evaluable on input columns
Projection must map to valid output columns
performance_impact: Standard implementation for all TopN queries; uses heap-based selection for efficiency; performance depends on whether input is pre-sorted; partitioned TopN enables parallel execution but requires proper data distribution
edge_cases: Zero limit returns empty result set
Offset exceeds input row count returns empty
Null values in order columns handled by OrderSpec null ordering
Partitioned TopN with single partition degenerates to global TopN
Split flag enables distributed TopN optimization
framework_notes: Implementation rule in Cascades framework; matches LOGICAL_TOPN pattern with PATTERN_LEAF child; creates single PhysicalTopN OptExpression preserving input children; does not directly trigger enforcers but PhysicalTopN operator may require sorted/distributed input properties enforced by separate EnforcerRules; rule fires during physical optimization phase when logical TopN needs physical implementation

summary: Physical implementation rule that converts LogicalAssertOneRowOperator to PhysicalAssertOneRowOperator, enabling runtime assertion that query returns exactly one row or within specified limits.
ra_condition: Direct 1:1 logical to physical mapping with no transformation
correctness_conditions: Child operator output must satisfy assertion constraint (exactly one row or within checkRows limit)
Assertion predicate must be evaluable on child output schema
All projected columns must be available from child operator
Limit constraint must be compatible with child cardinality
performance_impact: Minimal runtime overhead for assertion validation. Preferred when query semantics require single-row guarantee for correctness. No alternative implementations exist for this operator type.
edge_cases: Zero rows returned when one row expected triggers assertion failure
Multiple rows returned violating single-row constraint
NULL values in assertion predicate evaluation
Empty child result set with non-optional assertion
framework_notes: Cascades implementation rule matching LOGICAL_ASSERT_ONE_ROW pattern with PATTERN_LEAF child. Does not trigger enforcers directly. Matches group expressions with LogicalAssertOneRow at root. Rule fires during physical optimization phase when logical plan is being converted to physical operators.

summary: Transforms LogicalRawValuesOperator to PhysicalRawValuesOperator for executing VALUES clauses that generate constant row data without requiring child operators
ra_condition: No child operators or input dependencies required
correctness_conditions: Input operator must be LogicalRawValuesOperator
All constant data (RawConstantList) must be preserved
ColumnRefSet must match output schema
ConstantType must be correctly transferred
performance_impact: Negligible overhead - this is the only implementation for raw values, no alternative physical operators exist for this logical operation
edge_cases: Very large constant lists may impact memory
NULL value handling in constant list
framework_notes: Simple 1:1 transformation rule in Cascades framework, no enforcer insertion needed as leaf operator has no children, matches group expressions containing LogicalRawValuesOperator pattern, does not control memo explore depth as it's a terminal implementation rule

summary: Transforms a LogicalTableFunctionOperator into a PhysicalTableFunctionOperator, enabling the execution of table-valued functions (UDTFs) in the physical execution plan.
ra_condition: OperatorType matches LOGICAL_TABLE_FUNCTION with PATTERN_LEAF children
correctness_conditions: Function signature matches provided parameters
Outer column references (Lateral) are resolved by input schema
Result column references are valid and typed correctly
performance_impact: Provides the standard execution path for table functions; performance scales linearly with input cardinality and function complexity.
edge_cases: Function returns zero rows for specific input parameters
High memory usage if function generates large output per input row
framework_notes: Matches specific logical operator in Memo groups. Does not explicitly insert Enforcers (Sort/Exchange); physical property requirements are propagated by the optimizer's requirement enforcement phase.
stats_dependencies: Parameter column NDV and null ratio

summary: Transforms a Logical CTE Anchor operator into a Physical No CTE Operator, indicating the CTE reference should be executed without materialization (e.g., inlined).
ra_condition: CTE is eligible for non-materialized execution
correctness_conditions: CTE semantics must be preserved without materialization
Child plan (input index 1) contains valid execution logic
CTE is not required to be materialized by other references
performance_impact: Eliminates CTE materialization and scanning overhead, preferred for single-use or small CTEs.
edge_cases: CTEs referenced multiple times may force materialization elsewhere
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_ANCHOR with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF. Selects second input child as the physical plan. Part of Cascades Physical Implementation phase.

summary: Implementation rule that converts LogicalTableFunctionTableScanOperator to PhysicalTableFunctionTableScanOperator for table function-based table scans in StarRocks CBO
ra_condition: Table.Type == TABLE_FUNCTION
correctness_conditions: Table type must be TABLE_FUNCTION
Column references must map to valid column metadata
Predicates must be evaluable by table function
Limit and projection must be preservable
performance_impact: Primary implementation for table function scans; no alternative physical operators exist for this logical pattern; performance depends on table function implementation efficiency
edge_cases: Table function returns empty result set
Complex predicates that cannot be pushed to table function
Limit pushdown may affect table function execution
Projection column mismatch with table function output schema
framework_notes: Direct implementation rule in Cascades framework; matches LogicalTableFunctionTableScanOperator group expressions; does not trigger enforcer insertion; creates single OptExpression with physical operator; rule type IMP_TABLE_FUNCTION_TABLE_LSCAN_TO_PSCAN indicates logical-to-physical scan conversion

summary: Implementation rule that converts a LogicalSchemaScanOperator into a PhysicalSchemaScanOperator, enabling the execution of metadata or system table scans within the Cascades optimizer.
ra_condition: None
correctness_conditions: Input operator must be instance of LogicalSchemaScanOperator
Target schema table must exist and be accessible
No child operators required (Leaf node)
performance_impact: Low latency implementation required for INFORMATION_SCHEMA queries; avoids unnecessary data movement as metadata is local to FE/catalog.
edge_cases: Concurrent DDL modifying metadata during scan
High cardinality system tables (e.g. many partitions)
Missing statistics for custom system views
framework_notes: Matches leaf group expressions in Memo; creates a single physical alternative; does not recurse to children as it is a scan operator.

summary: Physical implementation rule that converts LogicalDeltaLakeScanOperator to PhysicalDeltaLakeScanOperator for Delta Lake table access in StarRocks Cascades CBO framework.
ra_condition: Delta Lake table metadata accessible and scan configuration valid
correctness_conditions: Delta Lake table exists and is accessible
Delta Log metadata can be read successfully
Scan predicates are compatible with Delta Lake filtering
Required columns exist in Delta Lake schema
Table version is consistent during scan
performance_impact: Primary and only implementation for Delta Lake scans; performance depends on file pruning efficiency, partition pruning, predicate pushdown to Delta Lake, and parallel scan execution. No alternative scan implementations exist for comparison.
edge_cases: Empty Delta Lake table with no files
Missing or corrupted Delta Log entries
Time travel queries to historical versions
framework_notes: Cascades-style implementation rule (RuleType.IMP_DELTALAKE_LSCAN_TO_PSCAN) matching LOGICAL_DELTALAKE_SCAN operator pattern. Creates single OptExpression with PhysicalDeltaLakeScanOperator. Does not trigger enforcer insertion directly in this rule - property enforcement handled separately. Matches group expressions containing LogicalDeltaLakeScanOperator. Rule fires during physical optimization phase when logical operators need physical implementations.

summary: Transforms a LogicalCTEProduceOperator into a PhysicalCTEProduceOperator, enabling physical execution of Common Table Expression definitions within the Cascades optimizer.
ra_input_pattern: LogicalCTEProduce (Custom Operator, semantically similar to ρ)
ra_condition: Input operator type matches LOGICAL_CTE_PRODUCE
correctness_conditions: Valid CTE ID exists in optimizer context
Child operators provide schema compatible with CTE definition
CTE definition is complete before consumption
performance_impact: Enables CTE materialization strategies; preferred when CTE is referenced multiple times to avoid recomputation, but adds materialization overhead if referenced once.
edge_cases: CTE with no consumers (dead code elimination)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_PRODUCE pattern in Implementation phase. Does not explicitly add enforcers; relies on framework property enforcement for distribution/ordering requirements.

summary: Transforms a Logical File Scan operator into a Physical File Scan operator, enabling the execution engine to read data from external file sources during the physical planning phase.
ra_condition: File source configuration is valid and accessible
correctness_conditions: File path exists and is readable
Schema definition matches file structure
User has necessary read permissions
performance_impact: Base I/O operation; parallelism determined by file split count; preferred for external data access without network shuffle overhead
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_FILE_SCAN group expressions. Acts as a leaf node terminating Memo exploration for this branch. Does not insert Enforcers; property enforcement is handled by separate EnforcerRules if parent requirements mismatch.

summary: Converts a Logical Iceberg Equality Delete Scan operator to its Physical counterpart, enabling Merge-on-Read (MOR) execution for Iceberg tables handling equality delete files.
ra_condition: OperatorType == LOGICAL_ICEBERG_EQUALITY_DELETE_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Table format is Iceberg
Equality delete files exist or MOR logic is valid
Scan predicates are pushable
MOR parameters are correctly initialized
performance_impact: Ensures correctness for Iceberg equality deletes; adds read-time overhead for merging deletes compared to copy-on-write or pre-compacted scans.
framework_notes: Standard Cascades Implementation Rule. Adds physical OptExpression to the current Group. Leaf node (Scan), terminates recursion for this branch. Property enforcement handled by framework.

summary: Implements Logical CTE Consume as a Physical No CTE Operator to enable CTE inlining when materialization is not explicitly forced by the context.
ra_condition: !context.getCteContext().isForceCTE(consume.getCteId())
correctness_conditions: CTE ID is not marked as forced materialization
CTE Definition is logically equivalent and available for inlining
performance_impact: Avoids CTE materialization and lookup overhead. Preferred for single-use CTEs. May cause redundant computation if CTE is referenced multiple times without forcing materialization.
edge_cases: CTE with volatile functions (may affect semantics if inlined)
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule. Matches LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME root. Gated by CteContext.isForceCTE. Does not explicitly inject Enforcers; property enforcement handled by framework if required properties mismatch.

summary: Converts a Logical Paimon Scan operator into a Physical Paimon Scan operator, establishing the execution path for reading data from Paimon data lake tables.
ra_condition: Input operator type is LOGICAL_PAIMON_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Paimon table metadata is accessible
Logical schema matches physical table schema
Snapshot ID or timestamp is valid
performance_impact: Enables Paimon table access; efficiency relies on internal partition pruning and predicate pushdown implemented in the physical operator.
framework_notes: Implementation rule matching LOGICAL_PAIMON_SCAN pattern. As a leaf node, it has no children to enforce properties on. Optimizer wraps result with Enforcers if parent requires specific distribution or ordering.

summary: Transforms a Logical ODPS Scan operator into a Physical ODPS Scan operator, enabling actual data retrieval from ODPS storage during query execution.
ra_condition: Pattern match on LogicalOdpsScanOperator
correctness_conditions: ODPS connector is configured and available
Target table exists in ODPS
User has read permissions on the ODPS table
Input OptExpression contains LogicalOdpsScanOperator
performance_impact: Essential for accessing ODPS data; performance depends on ODPS side filtering, projection pushdown, and network transfer costs.
edge_cases: Schema mismatch between catalog and ODPS
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_ODPS_SCAN in Memo groups. As a leaf node rule, it does not recurse to children. Property enforcement (distribution/order) is handled by parent operators or enforcer rules matching against this output.

summary: Transforms a Logical CTE Consume operator into a Physical CTE Consume operator, finalizing the reference to a shared CTE producer by removing tree children and linking via CTE ID.
ra_condition: OperatorType matches LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME
correctness_conditions: CTE ID must reference a valid, optimized CTE Producer in the OptimizerContext
CTE Output Column Reference Map must align with Producer schema
Limit and Predicate must be applicable to the CTE output
performance_impact: Enables CTE materialization and reuse; avoids redundant computation for multi-reference CTEs compared to inlining, but may incur materialization storage cost.
edge_cases: CTE Producer not yet optimized in Memo
CTE inlining optimization bypassing this rule
framework_notes: Implementation Rule (IMP_CTE_CONSUME_REUSE). Matches LogicalCTEConsume pattern. Children are explicitly dropped as CTE linkage is managed via ID in Memo, not expression tree edges. Does not directly invoke Enforcers.

summary: Transforms a Logical Aggregation operator into a Physical Hash Aggregate operator, enabling hash-based grouping and aggregation execution within the Cascades optimizer framework.
ra_condition: Applicable when the aggregation can be implemented using a hash table (most standard aggregations).
correctness_conditions: Grouping keys must be hashable (support equality and hash code)
Aggregation functions must support hash aggregation semantics
Memory budget must accommodate hash table or spill mechanism must be enabled
Data skew information must be accurately propagated if used for optimization
performance_impact: Preferred for unsorted data with moderate cardinality grouping keys. Offers O(1) average lookup time but consumes more memory than Sort Aggregation. Trade-off involves memory pressure vs. sorting overhead. Skew handling (via setDistinctColumnDataSkew) mitigates performance degradation on uneven data distribution.
edge_cases: High cardinality grouping keys causing memory spill to disk
Severe data skew leading to straggler tasks in distributed execution
Handling of NULL values in grouping columns (must hash consistently)
framework_notes: Matches LogicalAggr in Memo groups. Does not directly insert Enforcers; required properties (e.g., hash distribution) are satisfied by the optimizer framework via separate EnforcePropertyRules if the PhysicalHashAggregateOperator declares specific requirements. Controls Memo depth by adding a physical candidate to the group. Preserves child OptExpressions without modification.
stats_dependencies: NDV (Number of Distinct Values) of grouping keys

summary: This rule implements the physical execution strategy for Iceberg metadata scans by transforming the logical operator into a physical operator. It serves as a leaf node implementation in the Cascades optimizer, enabling queries against Iceberg system tables like files, partitions, and manifests.
ra_condition: OperatorType matches LOGICAL_ICEBERG_METADATA_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Iceberg catalog connection is valid
Target table exists and is an Iceberg table
User has permissions to read metadata tables
performance_impact: This is the mandatory implementation for metadata introspection. It is lightweight compared to data scans but depends on the number of metadata files (manifests) which can grow large.
edge_cases: Empty Iceberg tables with no manifests
Schema evolution causing metadata parsing errors
Missing statistics in Iceberg catalog leading to default cost estimates
framework_notes: Implementation Rule (IMP). Matches leaf logical expressions in Memo groups. Does not recurse to children. Cost and properties are computed by the Physical Operator instance, not the rule itself.

summary: Transforms Logical MySQL Scan to Physical MySQL Scan operator for external MySQL table access in StarRocks CBO framework
ra_condition: OperatorType.LOGICAL_MYSQL_SCAN
correctness_conditions: MySQL table must be accessible
Valid credentials and connection configured
Table schema matches logical operator definition
MySQL JDBC driver available
performance_impact: Primary and only implementation for MySQL external tables; performance depends on network latency, MySQL server load, and predicate pushdown effectiveness
edge_cases: Missing MySQL table statistics defaults to estimated costs
Large result sets without proper limit pushdown
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_MYSQL_SCAN group expressions in Memo; creates single physical alternative per logical expression; enforcer insertion handled separately by PropertyEnforcer rules not this implementation rule

summary: Transforms a LogicalValuesOperator into a PhysicalValuesOperator, implementing the execution of constant row sets (e.g., SQL VALUES clause) as a leaf node in the physical execution plan.
ra_condition: Operator type matches LogicalValuesOperator
correctness_conditions: Input OptExpression must contain LogicalValuesOperator
Row data structure must align with ColumnRefSet
Operator is a leaf node (no children)
performance_impact: Lowest cost implementation for value sets. Preferred always for LogicalValues. Large inline data may increase planning memory overhead.
framework_notes: Matches leaf group expressions in Memo. Returns OptExpression with empty child list. Does not enforce distribution/ordering; Enforcer rules wrap this output if specific physical properties are required by the parent.

summary: Transforms a Logical Intersect operator into a Physical Intersect operator, enabling the execution of set intersection operations within the Cascades optimizer framework.
ra_condition: Children match PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF (valid optimized sub-plans)
correctness_conditions: Child schemas must be compatible (same column count and types)
Input must be a LogicalSetOperator (Intersect)
Children plans must be valid physical candidates
performance_impact: Enables native Intersect execution; performance depends on data distribution and memory for hash tables; preferred over Join-based rewriting for clarity and potential optimization.
edge_cases: NULL handling (SQL standard treats NULLs as equal in INTERSECT)
Duplicate handling (INTERSECT ALL vs INTERSECT DISTINCT)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_INTERSECT in Memo groups. Does not explicitly insert Exchange/Sort; relies on framework property enforcement or operator internal handling.

summary: Implementation rule converting Logical Kudu Scan operator to Physical Kudu Scan operator, enabling execution of scans against Kudu storage tables.
ra_condition: True (Matches any Logical Kudu Scan operator)
correctness_conditions: Target table is a valid Kudu table
Kudu connector is initialized and accessible
Logical operator schema matches physical table schema
Required columns exist in Kudu table
performance_impact: Primary access path for Kudu data. Efficiency depends on predicate pushdown to Kudu and tablet pruning. No shuffle required if data distribution matches query requirements.
edge_cases: Schema evolution mismatch between catalog and storage
Complex predicates not supported by Kudu filter pushdown
framework_notes: Implementation rule adding physical alternative to Memo group. Leaf node rule, does not recurse on children. Costing happens during GroupExpression evaluation in the Optimizer.

summary: Transforms a Logical Iceberg Scan operator into a Physical Iceberg Scan operator, enabling execution of scans on Iceberg tables with support for Merge-On-Read (MOR) parameters.
ra_condition: Operator type must match LOGICAL_ICEBERG_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Iceberg catalog and table metadata are accessible
MOR (Merge-On-Read) parameters are valid if specified
Schema mapping between logical and physical is consistent
performance_impact: Essential for Iceberg table queries; introduces overhead for metadata resolution and MOR processing compared to native OLAP scans; benefits from partition pruning and file indexing.
edge_cases: High volume of delete vectors in MOR mode
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions containing LogicalIcebergScanOperator. As a leaf node rule, it terminates downward exploration. Property enforcement (Distribution/Ordering) is handled by separate Enforcer rules wrapping this operator if required by parents.

summary: Transforms a LogicalCTEAnchorOperator into a PhysicalCTEAnchorOperator, binding the CTE ID and consumption count for physical execution planning.
ra_condition: CTE ID is valid and registered in OptimizerContext
correctness_conditions: CTE ID must exist in CteContext
Consume number must be retrieved successfully
Input plan structure must match CTE definition requirements
performance_impact: Enables shared CTE execution plan; facilitates materialization decisions based on consume count to avoid redundant computation.
edge_cases: CTE consumed only once (potential inlining candidate)
CTE consumed zero times (should be pruned prior to this rule)
framework_notes: Matches Pattern.create(OperatorType.LOGICAL_CTE_ANCHOR, OperatorType.PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF). Implementation rule does not enforce properties; relies on framework enforcer rules for distribution/ordering satisfaction.

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical Hudi Scan operator into a Physical Hudi Scan operator, enabling the optimizer to select physical execution strategies for accessing Hudi tables.
ra_condition: OperatorType matches LOGICAL_HUDI_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Hudi catalog metadata is accessible
Logical schema matches physical table schema
Hudi timeline is consistent for read
performance_impact: Enables query execution on Hudi tables; performance depends on Hudi file layout, compaction state, and partition pruning efficiency compared to native OLAP scans.
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule (IMP). Matches leaf logical operator pattern. Adds physical expression to the Memo group without recursing to children. Costing occurs during group expression evaluation phase.

summary: Converts a Logical Repeat operator to a Physical Repeat operator, enabling the execution of GROUP BY REPEAT semantics by directly mapping logical properties to physical execution.
ra_condition: Child operators are successfully implemented as physical plans
correctness_conditions: Input schema must contain all repeat column references
Grouping IDs must be valid subsets of output columns
Child physical operators must satisfy distribution requirements
performance_impact: Minimal overhead as it is a direct mapping. Performance scales linearly with input size and number of grouping sets defined in the REPEAT clause.
edge_cases: Single grouping set (degenerates to standard GROUP BY)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_REPEAT in the Memo. Relies on the Cascades framework's property enforcement mechanism to insert Exchange/Sort enforcers if the PhysicalRepeatOperator requires specific distribution or ordering not met by children.

summary: Implementation rule converting Logical JDBC Scan operator to Physical JDBC Scan operator to enable data retrieval from external JDBC sources.
ra_condition: True (Always applicable for Logical JDBC Scan)
correctness_conditions: JDBC Catalog is configured
Remote Table exists and is accessible
Column schema mapping is valid
performance_impact: Mandatory implementation for JDBC tables; performance is bound by network latency and remote database query execution time.
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule; matches LOGICAL_JDBC_SCAN pattern; leaf node rule that adds physical expression to Memo group without transforming children.

summary: Transforms a LogicalFilterOperator into a PhysicalFilterOperator, preserving predicates, limits, and projections while wrapping the physical input plan.
ra_condition: Input operator must be LogicalFilter with any child operator
correctness_conditions: Predicate expressions must be valid for input schema
Physical operator must support predicate evaluation
No side-effects in predicate expressions
performance_impact: Standard filter execution path. Preferred when filter cannot be pushed down to scan. Separate operator adds minor overhead compared to fused ScanFilter but allows reuse of filter logic across different data sources.
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter in Memo groups. Generates PhysicalFilter alternative. Does not enforce properties but passes required properties to children. Part of the standard implementation rule set for logical-to-physical translation.

summary: Transforms a Logical Project operator into a Physical Project operator, preserving the column reference mapping and child inputs without altering data distribution.
ra_condition: Child operator X must have a valid physical implementation.
correctness_conditions: ColumnRefMap keys exist in child output schema
Expressions are type-compatible with target columns
Child physical plan is valid
performance_impact: Low overhead operator. Essential for column pruning and computed columns. No data shuffle or sort introduced.
framework_notes: Matches Pattern(LOGICAL_PROJECT, PATTERN_LEAF). Adds PhysicalProject to Memo group. Relies on separate Enforcer rules for distribution/ordering requirements.

summary: Transforms a Logical Hive Scan operator into a Physical Hive Scan operator, enabling the execution engine to read data from Hive tables.
ra_condition: OperatorType == LOGICAL_HIVE_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Hive table metadata is accessible
Logical schema matches physical table schema
User has read permissions on the Hive location
performance_impact: Defines the base access path for Hive data; performance depends on file format efficiency and predicate pushdown capabilities within the physical operator.
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions with LogicalHiveScanOperator. Does not enforce properties; EnforcerRules wrap this output if specific distribution or ordering is required by parent operators.

summary: Converts LogicalMetaScanOperator to PhysicalMetaScanOperator, enabling physical execution of metadata table scans within the Cascades optimizer.
ra_condition: Match OperatorType.LOGICAL_META_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Target metadata table exists
Session context is valid
No schema changes during planning
performance_impact: Essential for system metadata queries; negligible overhead compared to data scans due to small metadata volume.
framework_notes: Registers physical alternative in Memo. Does not handle property enforcement (Sort/Exchange), which is managed by separate Enforcer rules.

summary: This is an abstract base class for all physical implementation rules in StarRocks Cascades optimizer, not a concrete rule. It establishes that implementation rules have higher promise (priority=2) than transformation rules, ensuring physical operators are selected after logical transformations complete.
ra_condition: Subclass-specific pattern matching on logical expression
correctness_conditions: Subclass must define valid logical-to-physical operator mapping
Child groups must satisfy required physical properties
Implementation must preserve semantic equivalence
performance_impact: Base class provides no specific performance characteristics. Concrete implementations determine whether hash join, merge join, broadcast, shuffle, etc. is used based on cost model.
edge_cases: Abstract class cannot be instantiated directly
Promise value of 2 ensures implementation rules fire after transformation rules (promise=1)
Actual behavior depends entirely on concrete subclass implementation
framework_notes: This base class controls rule priority through promise()=2. In Cascades framework, implementation rules match logical group expressions and produce physical alternatives. Enforcer insertion is handled by separate PropertyEnforcer rules, not this base class. Memo explore depth is controlled by the optimizer task queue prioritization based on promise values.

summary: Transforms a Logical EXCEPT set operator into a Physical EXCEPT operator, enabling the execution of set difference operations within the Cascades optimization framework.
ra_condition: Child schemas must be union-compatible; matches LOGICAL_EXCEPT with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF children.
correctness_conditions: Input schemas are union-compatible (same column count and types)
Null handling semantics match SQL standard
Data distribution aligned (hash shuffled) for distributed execution
performance_impact: High computational cost due to deduplication and comparison; requires network shuffle in distributed mode which increases latency.
edge_cases: Null value handling in comparison columns
High data skew affecting shuffle performance
framework_notes: Implementation Rule (IMP_EXCEPT); matches LOGICAL_EXCEPT pattern; relies on Optimizer context for property enforcement (Exchange/Sort) rather than triggering Enforcers directly.

summary: Converts Logical Union to Physical Union, preserving union-all semantics and potentially absorbing limit, predicate, and projection operations.
ra_condition: Inputs must have compatible schemas and column mappings
correctness_conditions: Child schemas must match union output schema
Column types must be compatible
Child output columns mapping must be valid
performance_impact: Union All is O(N) concatenation. Absorbing predicate/limit reduces upstream operator count. Distinct requires deduplication.
framework_notes: Matches LogicalUnion in Memo groups. Does not enforce distribution; relies on separate Enforcer rules for Exchange insertion.

summary: Transforms a LogicalWindowOperator into a PhysicalWindowOperator, finalizing the execution strategy for analytic functions based on pre-determined partitioning (hash/sort) and sorting requirements.
ra_input_pattern: ω(R) where R is any relational input (PATTERN_LEAF)
ra_output_pattern: ω_phys(R) representing the physical execution of window functions
ra_condition: Logical window specification is valid and supported by physical execution engine
correctness_conditions: Input schema contains all partition and order by columns
Window function arguments are type-compatible
Memory budget suffices for window buffer accumulation
Partitioning strategy (hash/sort) matches data distribution requirements
performance_impact: Enables analytic function execution; hash-based partitioning preferred for large unsorted datasets, sort-based preferred if input is already ordered
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_WINDOW + PATTERN_LEAF in Memo. Adds PhysicalWindow as a candidate plan in the GroupExpression. Property enforcement (Sort/Exchange) is handled by separate EnforcerRules triggered by the physical operator's requirements.

summary: Transforms a Logical Join operator into a Physical Join operator (e.g., Hash, Merge, NestedLoop) by evaluating equality predicates and join types to select the optimal execution strategy within the Cascades framework.
ra_condition: Valid join predicates exist; Join type supported by physical operator; Equi-predicates required for Hash/Merge
correctness_conditions: Equi-predicates must exist for Hash/Merge Join
Join keys must be compatible types
Memory must suffice for Hash Build phase (if Hash Join)
Input distribution must match Physical Join requirements
performance_impact: Hash Join is default for large datasets; Merge Join preferred if inputs are pre-sorted to avoid shuffle; Nested Loop for small outer tables. Incorrect selection causes network spill or high CPU usage.
edge_cases: Skewed data distribution causing shuffle imbalance
Missing statistics leading to suboptimal plan
framework_notes: Matches Logical Join GroupExpression in Memo. Children use PATTERN_LEAF to allow any optimized plan. Triggers Exchange/Sort Enforcers to satisfy Physical Join distribution/ordering requirements defined by the chosen physical operator.

summary: Transforms a Logical Elasticsearch Scan operator into a Physical Elasticsearch Scan operator, enabling the execution engine to retrieve data from external Elasticsearch indices.
ra_condition: OperatorType == LOGICAL_ES_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Elasticsearch cluster is reachable
Index mapping matches StarRocks schema
Predicate pushdown expressions are valid ES DSL
performance_impact: Enables federated query capability; performance hinges on network bandwidth and ES query optimization (filter pushdown).
edge_cases: Schema Drift between ES and StarRocks
framework_notes: Leaf node implementation rule. Matches specific group expressions in Memo. Does not explore children. Property enforcement (Exchange/Sort) is handled by separate Enforcer rules if required properties mismatch.

summary: Transforms LogicalTopN operator to PhysicalTopN operator, implementing top-N selection with ordering, limit, offset, and optional partitioning capabilities in the physical execution layer.
ra_input_pattern: λ(τ(R)) - Logical TopN with ordering over relation R
ra_output_pattern: PhysicalTopN - Physical implementation of top-N selection with OrderSpec
ra_condition: Child operator must match PATTERN_LEAF (any physical operator)
correctness_conditions: OrderSpec must preserve logical ordering semantics
Limit and offset values must be non-negative
Partition columns must be valid for distribution if partitioned TopN
Sort phase must be compatible with execution context
performance_impact: Preferred when direct TopN execution is more efficient than sort+limit; supports partitioned TopN for distributed execution reducing data shuffle; trade-off vs full sort when limit is small relative to input size
edge_cases: Offset exceeds row count returns empty
Null handling in order columns requires consistent null ordering
Partition limit with uneven data distribution may cause skew
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_TOPN with PATTERN_LEAF child in Cascades memo group; enforcer insertion occurs when child output properties (ordering/distribution) don't match PhysicalTopN requirements; rule fires during physical optimization phase after logical transformations complete

summary: Transforms a LogicalOlapScanOperator into a PhysicalOlapScanOperator, enabling physical execution of OLAP table scans within the StarRocks CBO framework.
ra_condition: OperatorType.LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Underlying OLAP table metadata is valid
Transaction snapshot is active
Column access paths (indices) are valid
performance_impact: Primary data access path. Performance depends on predicate pushdown and index selection configured in the Logical operator prior to this rule.
framework_notes: Leaf node rule in Cascades framework. Matches group expressions containing LogicalOlapScanOperator. Copies Salt and AccessPaths properties from Logical to Physical operator. Does not recurse to children.

summary: Transforms LogicalLimitOperator to PhysicalLimitOperator during implementation phase, ensuring only global limits are processed in the physical execution plan
ra_condition: limit.isGlobal() == true
correctness_conditions: Limit must be global (not local) to avoid distributed execution issues
Input pattern must match LOGICAL_LIMIT with leaf children
Offset and limit values must be non-negative
performance_impact: Minimal overhead - direct operator conversion with no alternative physical implementations available for limit
edge_cases: Large offset values may impact performance
framework_notes: Matches LogicalLimit with PATTERN_LEAF in Cascades memo structure, does not trigger enforcer insertion as limit operator preserves child distribution and ordering properties without modification

summary: Transforms LogicalAssertOneRowOperator into PhysicalAssertOneRowOperator, enabling physical execution of row count assertions while preserving child plan structure.
ra_condition: OperatorType is LOGICAL_ASSERT_ONE_ROW; Child matches PATTERN_LEAF
correctness_conditions: Input plan must be valid physical plan
Runtime row count must satisfy assertion constraints (e.g., <= 1 row)
performance_impact: Minimal CPU overhead for validation; ensures semantic correctness for scalar contexts without altering data flow.
edge_cases: Input produces 0 rows (may pass or fail depending on assertion type)
framework_notes: Matches specific logical operator in Memo Group. Does not enforce properties; relies on Cascades framework to insert Enforcers (Sort/Exchange) if child properties mismatch requirements.

summary: Transforms LogicalRawValuesOperator to PhysicalRawValuesOperator for generating constant value rows without scanning any table, implementing the VALUES clause execution
ra_condition: No child operators required (leaf node)
correctness_conditions: Logical operator must be LogicalRawValuesOperator
Column reference set must be preserved
Constant types must match output schema
Raw constant list must be non-null and valid
Constant count must be consistent with schema
performance_impact: Always preferred as the only implementation for VALUES clause; negligible cost compared to table scans; memory usage scales with constant list size
edge_cases: Type conversion requirements between logical and physical
Large constant lists causing memory pressure
framework_notes: Leaf node rule with no children; no enforcer insertion needed since no distribution/ordering requirements; matches single group expression with LogicalRawValuesOperator pattern; does not control memo explore depth as it's a terminal rule

summary: Transforms a LogicalTableFunctionOperator into a PhysicalTableFunctionOperator, enabling the execution of table functions (e.g., unnest, explode) in the physical plan.
ra_condition: Pattern match on OperatorType.LOGICAL_TABLE_FUNCTION with PATTERN_LEAF child
correctness_conditions: Table function parameters are resolvable columns from input
Function return type matches expected schema
Input child plan is valid
performance_impact: Direct implementation of table functions; performance depends on function complexity and row expansion factor. No alternative physical strategies (like split/merge) are shown in this rule.
edge_cases: Table function returns empty set for some rows
framework_notes: Matches OperatorType.LOGICAL_TABLE_FUNCTION. Adds a physical alternative to the Memo group. Does not explicitly handle distribution or sorting requirements; enforced by framework property enforcement phase.

summary: Transforms a Logical CTE Anchor into a Physical No CTE Operator, bypassing CTE materialization and sharing mechanisms by directly executing the consumer plan branch.
ra_condition: Applicable when the optimizer decides CTE sharing is unnecessary (e.g., single reference) or disabled for the specific CTE ID.
correctness_conditions: CTE ID is valid and exists in context
Consumer plan (input 1) is semantically complete without CTE sharing
No recursive dependencies requiring materialization
performance_impact: Reduces memory and IO overhead associated with CTE materialization; preferred for single-use CTEs to avoid unnecessary storage and lookup costs.
edge_cases: Recursive CTEs generally cannot use this path
Multi-reference CTEs may lead to redundant computation if this rule is chosen over sharing
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule (IMP); adds a physical alternative to the Memo group for the Logical Anchor; does not enforce distribution or ordering properties.

summary: Implementation rule that converts LogicalTableFunctionTableScanOperator to PhysicalTableFunctionTableScanOperator when the underlying table is a TABLE_FUNCTION type, enabling execution of table-valued functions in the query plan
ra_condition: Table.Type == TABLE_FUNCTION
correctness_conditions: Table type must be TABLE_FUNCTION
Column references in ColRefToColumnMetaMap must be valid
Scan predicates must be applicable to table function output
Projection expressions must be computable from function output columns
performance_impact: This is the only physical implementation for table function scans; no alternative operators exist. Performance depends on table function implementation efficiency and predicate pushdown effectiveness
edge_cases: Table function returns empty result set
Complex predicates that cannot be pushed to table function
Limit clause interaction with table function evaluation
Table function with side effects or stateful execution
framework_notes: Direct one-to-one logical-to-physical conversion rule in Cascades framework; does not trigger Enforcer insertion as it's a leaf operator; matches group expressions containing LogicalTableFunctionTableScanOperator; rule fires during physical optimization phase when implementation rules are explored

summary: Transforms a LogicalSchemaScanOperator into a PhysicalSchemaScanOperator, providing the executable implementation for scanning system schema or external catalog tables.
ra_condition: Input operator type matches LogicalSchemaScanOperator
correctness_conditions: Input OptExpression contains LogicalSchemaScanOperator
Target schema table metadata is accessible
performance_impact: Mandatory leaf implementation; no alternative physical operators exist for this logical type, ensuring schema tables can be queried.
edge_cases: User lacks permission to access schema
Empty result set from system table
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions containing LogicalSchemaScan. Adds PhysicalSchemaScan OptExpression to the Memo. As a leaf rule, it does not recurse to children or trigger child enforcers.

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical Delta Lake Scan operator into a Physical Delta Lake Scan operator, enabling the execution engine to read data from Delta Lake tables.
ra_condition: OperatorType matches LOGICAL_DELTALAKE_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Delta Lake table metadata is valid and accessible
Logical operator contains correct snapshot ID
Storage credentials and connectivity are configured
performance_impact: Enables Delta Lake integration. Performance relies on file pruning, predicate pushdown, and network bandwidth. No alternative physical scan exists for this logical type.
edge_cases: Schema evolution in Delta Lake table
Missing statistics leading to suboptimal planning
framework_notes: Leaf node rule. Adds physical candidate to the Memo group containing the logical scan. Does not explore children. Property enforcement (e.g., Distribution) is handled by separate Enforcer rules if the physical scan does not satisfy parent requirements.

summary: Transforms a Logical CTE Produce operator into a Physical CTE Produce operator, establishing the physical execution boundary for Common Table Expression materialization.
ra_condition: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_PRODUCE operator type with multi-leaf pattern
correctness_conditions: CTE ID must be unique and consistent with CTE Consume operators
Output schema must match CTE definition
Input plan must be fully optimized
performance_impact: Enables CTE result materialization; preferred for multi-reference CTEs to avoid recomputation, but adds overhead for single-use CTEs compared to inlining
edge_cases: CTE inlining may bypass this rule entirely
framework_notes: Standard Cascades implementation rule; does not enforce distribution or ordering properties directly, relying on framework enforcers for property satisfaction between groups

summary: Transforms a LogicalFileScanOperator into a PhysicalFileScanOperator, enabling the execution of file-based data scans in the physical plan.
ra_condition: input.op.type == LOGICAL_FILE_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Target file exists and is readable
File format parser is available
Logical schema matches physical file schema
Predicate pushdown constraints are satisfied
performance_impact: Essential for external table queries. Efficiency relies on columnar format pruning and parallel split scheduling.
framework_notes: Leaf rule in the Cascades memo. Does not recurse into children. Property enforcement (distribution/ordering) is resolved by ancestors or exchange operators inserted above this node.

summary: This rule provides physical implementation for CTE (Common Table Expression) consumption by converting LogicalCTEConsumeOperator to PhysicalNoCTEOperator when CTE materialization is not forced, enabling CTE inlining optimization.
ra_condition: CTE is not forced to materialize (!isForceCTE)
correctness_conditions: CTE must not be forced to materialize via context flag
CTE definition must be available in CteContext for inlining
Projection columns from logical operator must be preserved in physical operator
CTE ID must be maintained for proper plan tracking
performance_impact: Preferred when CTE is referenced few times (avoids materialization overhead). Trade-off: inlining saves memory but may cause recomputation if CTE is used multiple times. Materialized CTE alternative better for high-reuse scenarios.
edge_cases: CTE used multiple times may cause plan explosion with inlining
CTE with side effects or non-deterministic functions may need materialization
Large CTE result sets may exceed memory if inlined repeatedly
framework_notes: Cascades-style implementation rule matching LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME pattern. Does not trigger enforcer insertion directly. Controls memo exploration by providing alternative physical implementation alongside materialized CTE options. Matches group expressions where CTE consume is root operator with leaf children.

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical ODPS Scan operator into a Physical ODPS Scan operator, enabling execution of external table scans from Alibaba ODPS within the StarRocks query plan.
ra_condition: Input operator is instance of LogicalOdpsScanOperator
correctness_conditions: ODPS project and table exist
User has read permissions on ODPS table
Logical schema matches physical ODPS schema
performance_impact: Essential for ODPS data access; performance relies on ODPS partition pruning and network bandwidth between StarRocks and ODPS.
edge_cases: Network timeouts during ODPS metadata fetch
framework_notes: Leaf node transformation rule. Matches specific GroupExpression containing LogicalOdpsScanOperator. Does not explore children as scans are leaves. Costing occurs after rule application.

summary: Implements Logical Aggregation using a Physical Hash Aggregate operator, converting logical grouping and aggregation semantics into a hash-based execution plan.
ra_condition: Matches any LogicalAggregationOperator pattern
correctness_conditions: Grouping keys must support hash equality
Memory quota must accommodate hash table size
Aggregation functions must be stateless or mergeable
performance_impact: Optimal for unsorted data with moderate cardinality; avoids sort overhead but risks memory spill on high cardinality compared to SortAgg.
edge_cases: Extreme data skew on hash keys
Memory exhaustion leading to disk spill
framework_notes: Populates Memo with PhysicalHashAggregate expression. Relies on PropertyConverter/Enforcer rules to satisfy distribution requirements (e.g., Hash Shuffle) via Exchange operators.

summary: Transforms a Logical Intersect operator into a Physical Intersect operator within the Cascades optimizer framework, enabling execution of set intersection logic.
ra_condition: Child operators must produce union-compatible schemas (identical column count, types, and order).
correctness_conditions: Input schemas must be identical (column count, types, order)
Null semantics must be preserved across inputs
Duplicate elimination logic must be consistent with SQL standard
performance_impact: High overhead due to potential data shuffling and deduplication; preferred for semantic correctness over Join-based emulation.
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions with LogicalIntersect and PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF children. Does not explicitly insert Enforcers; framework adds Exchange nodes via separate Enforcement Rules if PhysicalIntersect's distribution requirements are unmet.

summary: Transforms a Logical Kudu Scan operator into a Physical Kudu Scan operator, enabling actual data retrieval from Kudu storage during query execution.
ra_condition: Operator type is LOGICAL_KUDU_SCAN
correctness_conditions: Table metadata is valid
Kudu service is reachable
Column types match schema
performance_impact: Primary access path for Kudu tables; efficiency depends on predicate pushdown and range partition pruning capabilities within the physical operator
framework_notes: Matches leaf logical operator. Does not transform children. Physical properties (distribution/order) defined by operator determine if upstream Enforcers are needed.

summary: Transforms Logical CTE Anchor operator to Physical CTE Anchor operator, enabling CTE consumption tracking and materialization management during query execution in StarRocks Cascades optimizer
ra_condition: cteId must exist in CteContext with valid consumeNum tracking
correctness_conditions: CTE definition must be registered before anchor execution
consumeNum must match actual CTE reference count
cteId must be unique and valid in CteContext
Projection schema must be preserved through anchor
performance_impact: Negligible runtime overhead - enables CTE reuse without recomputation, critical for queries with multiple CTE references
edge_cases: consumeNum mismatch between definition and consumption
framework_notes: Implementation rule (RuleType.IMP_CTE_ANCHOR) in Cascades framework - matches LOGICAL_CTE_ANCHOR with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF pattern, creates single PhysicalCTEAnchorOperator without triggering enforcer insertion, operates during physical optimization phase

summary: Implementation rule that converts a Logical JDBC Scan operator into a Physical JDBC Scan operator, enabling execution of scans on external JDBC tables within the federated query framework.
ra_condition: OperatorType matches LOGICAL_JDBC_SCAN
correctness_conditions: JDBC catalog configuration is valid
Remote table schema matches logical schema
JDBC driver and connection are available
performance_impact: Mandatory implementation for JDBC tables. Performance is bound by network latency and remote database execution speed. No alternative physical operators exist for this logical type.
edge_cases: Type mapping mismatches between StarRocks and JDBC source
Large result set causing memory pressure
framework_notes: Standard Cascades Implementation Rule. Matches leaf logical node in Memo. Does not enforce distribution/order; relies on separate EnforcerRules if specific properties are required by parents.

summary: Transforms a Logical Filter operator into a Physical Filter operator, directly mapping predicates, limits, and projections without altering the plan structure or data distribution.
ra_condition: Child operator matches PATTERN_LEAF
correctness_conditions: Predicate columns exist in child output schema
Predicate data types are supported by Physical Filter
performance_impact: Minimal CPU overhead for row filtering. Enables vectorized execution. Does not introduce network or disk I/O.
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter with any child (PATTERN_LEAF). Property enforcement (Sort/Exchange) is handled by the optimizer's enforcer rules, not this implementation rule.

summary: Converts a Logical Hive Scan operator into a Physical Hive Scan operator, establishing the execution path for reading data from Hive tables.
ra_condition: None (Always applicable for LogicalHiveScanOperator)
correctness_conditions: Hive table metadata is resolvable
Underlying file format is supported (e.g., PARQUET, ORC)
Logical schema matches physical table schema
performance_impact: Primary access method for Hive data; efficiency relies on partition pruning and predicate pushdown to reduce I/O.
framework_notes: Leaf rule in Cascades Memo. Adds PhysicalHiveScan to the same Group as LogicalHiveScan. Does not handle distribution/order enforcement; Enforcer rules match this output to insert Exchange/Sort if needed.

summary: Transforms a LogicalUnionOperator into a PhysicalUnionOperator, enabling execution of set union operations while preserving fused projection, predicate, and limit clauses.
ra_condition: Operator is LogicalUnion with children matching PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF
correctness_conditions: Input column schemas must be compatible (type and count)
Child output columns must align with union definition
Union All flag must be respected for deduplication logic
performance_impact: Efficient O(N) concatenation for Union All; fused predicates/projections reduce operator overhead; Distinct unions incur aggregation cost
edge_cases: Null value handling across mixed inputs
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions with LogicalUnion in Memo; generates Physical candidate without modifying children; Distribution/Sort enforcement handled by separate PropertyEnforcer rules.

summary: Base rule for converting Logical Join to Physical Join, extracting equality predicates to enable hash-based implementation strategies.
ra_condition: Join operator is LogicalJoin; Equality predicates exist for partitioning
correctness_conditions: Join semantics (Inner/Outer) must be preserved
Equality predicates must be non-null safe or handled correctly
Memory must accommodate hash table or spill mechanism active
performance_impact: Enables efficient Hash Join for equi-joins; incurs network shuffle cost. Preferred over NLJ for large datasets.
edge_cases: High skew on join keys leads to data imbalance
Missing statistics may cause wrong build/probe side selection
Non-equi predicates may force fallback to Nested Loop Join
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_JOIN in Memo groups. Triggers DistributionEnforcer for child alignment. Base class for specific join strategies like HashJoinImplementationRule.

summary: This rule implements the conversion of a Logical OLAP Scan operator into a Physical OLAP Scan operator, serving as the leaf node implementation for accessing native StarRocks tables.
ra_condition: None (Always applicable if pattern matches)
correctness_conditions: Table metadata must exist and be valid
User must have read permissions on the table
Column access paths must be compatible with table schema
performance_impact: Primary access path for StarRocks native OLAP tables; performance depends on partition pruning and index selection configured in ColumnAccessPaths.
edge_cases: Partition pruning already applied in logical phase
framework_notes: Matches OperatorType.LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN. As a leaf node, it has no children to explore. Enforcers (Exchange/Sort) are inserted by the framework above this node if required properties are not met.

summary: Transforms LogicalLimitOperator to PhysicalLimitOperator during the implementation phase of Cascades optimizer, ensuring only global limits are converted to physical execution operators
ra_condition: limit.isGlobal() == true
correctness_conditions: Limit operator must be global (not local/distributed)
Must be executed during implementation phase
Input expression must contain LogicalLimitOperator
Offset and limit values must be non-negative integers
performance_impact: Minimal overhead - direct operator conversion with no alternative physical implementations available for limit operation
edge_cases: Local limit operators will throw StarRocksPlannerException with INTERNAL_ERROR
Zero limit value may result in empty result set
Large offset values may cause performance degradation if not optimized upstream
framework_notes: Cascades implementation rule matching LogicalLimit with PATTERN_LEAF pattern, does not trigger enforcer insertion for sort/exchange, converts logical to physical in single transformation step without exploring alternative implementations

summary: Transforms a Logical CTE Anchor operator into a Physical CTE Anchor operator, embedding the CTE ID and consumption count from the optimizer context for physical execution planning.
ra_condition: CTE ID must exist in OptimizerContext CteContext
correctness_conditions: CTE ID must be valid and registered in context
Projection schema must be preserved from logical to physical
Child input expressions must remain unchanged
performance_impact: Enables physical execution of CTE definitions; allows downstream rules to decide materialization vs inlining based on consumeNum.
edge_cases: CTE consumed zero times (potential dead code elimination)
CTE consumed multiple times (requires shared scan or materialization)
framework_notes: Matches OperatorType.LOGICAL_CTE_ANCHOR pattern. Retrieves consumeNum via context.getCteContext(). Does not modify child inputs or trigger property enforcers directly.

summary: Transforms a LogicalJDBCScanOperator into a PhysicalJDBCScanOperator, enabling the execution of scans on external JDBC data sources within the physical plan.
ra_condition: None (Unconditional implementation rule for matching pattern)
correctness_conditions: JDBC catalog connection is active
Remote database supports pushed-down predicates
Schema mapping between StarRocks and remote DB is valid
performance_impact: Enables federated query capability. Performance depends heavily on network bandwidth and remote DB load. Pushing down filters is critical to reduce data transfer.
edge_cases: Predicate pushdown failure forcing local filtering
framework_notes: Cascades Implementation Rule (ImpRule). Matches group expressions containing LogicalJDBCScan. Adds physical alternative to the Memo group. Being a leaf node, it has no children to enforce properties on.

summary: Transforms a LogicalFilterOperator into a PhysicalFilterOperator, implementing predicate evaluation on input rows while preserving embedded limit and projection configurations.
ra_condition: Input operator type is LOGICAL_FILTER with any child operator
correctness_conditions: Predicate expression columns exist in input schema
Predicate evaluation is deterministic and side-effect free
SQL three-valued logic (TRUE/FALSE/NULL) is strictly preserved
performance_impact: Standard implementation for filtering; linear CPU cost relative to input cardinality; no data movement or shuffle overhead introduced.
edge_cases: Predicates evaluating to constant TRUE (passthrough) or FALSE (empty result)
NULL value handling in comparison operators
Embedded projection or limit logic within the filter operator
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_FILTER on PATTERN_LEAF (any child). Adds PhysicalFilter to Memo group. Propagates required properties (order/distribution) to child without enforcing new ones.

summary: Converts a Logical Hive Scan operator into a Physical Hive Scan operator, enabling the execution engine to read data from Hive tables.
ra_condition: Input OptExpression contains a LogicalHiveScanOperator
correctness_conditions: Hive table metadata is valid and accessible
Logical schema matches physical table schema
Required file formats (ORC/Parquet) are supported
performance_impact: Primary access path for Hive data; efficiency relies on predicate pushdown, partition pruning, and column projection.
edge_cases: Partition pruning resulting in no files
framework_notes: Leaf rule in Cascades; adds Physical OptExpression to the current Group; terminates exploration for this branch as scans have no children.

summary: Transforms a LogicalUnionOperator into a PhysicalUnionOperator, enabling physical execution of set union operations with support for fused projection, predicate, and limit clauses.
ra_condition: Operator type is LOGICAL_UNION with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF children
correctness_conditions: Child schemas must be union-compatible (same column count and types)
Column reference mappings must be valid
Union All flag must be correctly preserved
performance_impact: Efficient concatenation for Union All; fused operators reduce materialization overhead; avoids network shuffle if child distributions match
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions in Memo; Costing and Enforcer (Exchange/Sort) insertion are handled by separate framework components based on RequiredProperties

summary: Base CBO rule transforming Logical Join operators into Physical Join implementations (e.g., Hash, Merge) by extracting equi-predicates and preserving join semantics within the Cascades optimizer.
ra_condition: Valid join type and extractable equi-predicates for optimized strategies
correctness_conditions: Join type semantic equivalence (Inner/Left/Right/Full)
Predicate logical equivalence
Column reference validity across children
performance_impact: Determines distributed execution strategy (Shuffle vs Broadcast vs Local), directly impacting network I/O, memory consumption, and latency
edge_cases: Non-equi joins fallback to Nested Loop Join
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_JOIN in Memo groups; Physical operator requirements trigger Exchange/Sort Enforcer rules; Base class for specific strategies (Hash/Merge); Controls exploration depth via cost-based pruning

summary: Transforms LogicalOlapScanOperator to PhysicalOlapScanOperator for OLAP table access in StarRocks CBO, preserving salt configuration and column access paths
ra_condition: Valid OLAP table with accessible metadata and column definitions
correctness_conditions: Valid LogicalOlapScanOperator instance
Table metadata accessible
Column access paths valid
Salt configuration preserved
Tablet distribution information available
performance_impact: Default implementation for OLAP table scans; provides direct table access without additional transformation overhead; performance depends on column pruning and predicate pushdown
framework_notes: Cascades implementation rule matching LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN pattern (RuleType.IMP_OLAP_LSCAN_TO_PSCAN); copies salt and column access paths from logical to physical operator; no enforcer insertion at this rule level; matches group expressions containing LogicalOlapScanOperator

summary: Transforms LogicalLimitOperator to PhysicalLimitOperator during the implementation phase, ensuring only global limits are processed in the physical execution plan
ra_condition: limit.isGlobal() == true
correctness_conditions: Limit operator must be global (not local) - local limits throw StarRocksPlannerException
Input OptExpression must match LogicalLimit with leaf child pattern
Offset and limit values must be non-negative integers
Projection schema must be compatible with input schema
performance_impact: Direct logical-to-physical conversion with minimal overhead; no alternative physical implementations (e.g., distributed vs local) are considered in this rule; global limit ensures correct result semantics across distributed execution
edge_cases: Local limit operators in implementation phase cause internal error exception
Zero limit value returns empty result set
Offset larger than input row count returns empty result
Null projection handling depends on PhysicalLimitOperator constructor
framework_notes: Cascades implementation rule matching LogicalLimit with PATTERN_LEAF child pattern; operates during implementation phase on Memo group expressions; does not trigger Sort/Exchange enforcer insertion; rule type IMP_LIMIT indicates physical implementation transformation
